assertion_results.text: Test Quality verdict is WARN (not N/A or PASS) — the Eval Quality component is WARN because eval-3 has 2 failing assertions, and WARN in any component produces WARN in the combined Test Quality verdict
assertion_results.evidence: Report summary table row: '| Test Quality | WARN | Repetitive Test Detection: N/A (no test files). Test Documentation: N/A (no test files). Eval Quality: WARN -- eval-3 at 85% pass rate (2 regression failures: convention upgrade eligibility, sub-task creation for upgraded suggestions). Overall pass rate: 54/56 (96%). |'. Test Quality is WARN, driven by Eval Quality WARN.

assertion_results.text: The eval result review from github-actions[bot] is correctly identified as an eval result — the report's Test Quality or Eval Quality section reflects eval metrics (pass rates, failing assertions) extracted from the eval review, confirming the orchestrator detected and processed it
assertion_results.evidence: Report Eval Quality section (line 111): 'Eval result review detected from github-actions[bot] (review id 40001).' The section includes per-eval pass rate table (eval-1 through eval-5), overall pass rate 54/56 (96%), and two specific failing assertion texts with evidence from eval-3, confirming the eval review was detected and processed.

assertion_results.text: The human reviewer comment from reviewer-b (comment id 50001) is NOT misidentified as an eval result — it is processed as a normal review comment (classified in review-50001.md) and its classification does not reference eval detection or eval metrics
assertion_results.evidence: review-50001.md classifies comment 50001 as 'Code Change Request' based on reviewer-b's directive language and CHANGES_REQUESTED state. The file explicitly states 'This is NOT an eval result review because: The author is reviewer-b (a human reviewer), not github-actions[bot]; The body does not contain ## Eval Results; The body does not contain sdlc-workflow/run-evals'. No eval metrics appear in the classification reasoning.

assertion_results.text: Eval pass rate and failing assertion details appear in the verification report's Test Quality row — the report includes eval-3's failing assertions about convention upgrade eligibility and sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: Test Quality row includes 'eval-3 at 85% pass rate (2 regression failures: convention upgrade eligibility, sub-task creation for upgraded suggestions)'. The detailed Eval Quality section lists both failing assertions verbatim: (1) 'Convention upgrade eligibility is evaluated for review comment 30002...' with evidence about missing CONVENTIONS.md lookup, and (2) 'Review comment 30002 (index suggestion) results in a sub-task...' with evidence about no convention upgrade being attempted.

assertion_results.text: An eval failure sub-task is created for failing eval-3 — a sub-task description file targets eval-3 assertion failures and includes the failing assertion text and evidence
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-1.md title: 'Fix eval-3 assertion failures related to convention upgrade eligibility evaluation and sub-task creation for review comment 30002'. The Review Context section quotes both failing assertions with their evidence text. The Eval Failure Sub-Tasks table in the report confirms: '| eval-3 | 2 (convention upgrade eligibility, sub-task creation) | regression | Sub-task created -- fix eval-3 assertion failures |'.

assertion_results.text: Sub-task descriptions include Review Context with the failing assertion text and evidence from the eval review — the Review Context section quotes the two failing assertions about convention upgrade eligibility and sub-task creation, including the evidence text explaining what was missing
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-1.md Review Context section (lines 32-37) quotes both assertions with evidence: (1) Assertion about convention upgrade eligibility with evidence 'The output file review-30002.md classifies the comment as a suggestion but does not evaluate convention upgrade eligibility -- no CONVENTIONS.md lookup or codebase pattern analysis is documented in the classification reasoning', and (2) Assertion about sub-task creation with evidence 'No sub-task was created for review comment 30002 -- it was classified as suggestion and no convention upgrade was attempted, so the suggestion was not elevated to a code change request'.

assertion_results.text: Sub-task descriptions include a Target PR section pointing to the PR URL https://github.com/mrizzi/sdlc-plugins/pull/747 so that implement-task adds commits to the existing PR branch
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-1.md line 39: '## Target PR' followed by 'https://github.com/mrizzi/sdlc-plugins/pull/747'. subtask-2.md line 32: '## Target PR' followed by 'https://github.com/mrizzi/sdlc-plugins/pull/747'. Both sub-task descriptions include the correct Target PR URL.

assertion_results.text: Root-cause investigation runs on the created eval failure sub-tasks — the report includes a Root-Cause Investigation verdict that is not N/A, indicating the investigation pipeline processed the eval failure sub-tasks
assertion_results.evidence: Report summary table row: '| Root-Cause Investigation | DONE | Eval-3 regressions traced to implement-task phase: convention upgrade eligibility evaluation not performed for suggestion-classified comments |'. The verdict is DONE (not N/A). The Root-Cause Investigation section (lines 156-163) provides detailed analysis including universality test, method-vs-fact test, classification as 'Skill gap in the implement-task phase', and a preventive fix recommendation.

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, creates sub-task descriptions, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: All output files are verification/reporting artifacts: report.md (verification report), criterion-1.md through criterion-7.md (acceptance criteria reasoning), review-50001.md (review classification), subtask-1.md and subtask-2.md (sub-task descriptions). No code files were created or modified -- all outputs are documentation/reporting files in the outputs/ directory.

assertion_results.text: Non-eval checks (Scope Containment, Sensitive Patterns, Acceptance Criteria) are unaffected by eval processing — Scope Containment compares the diff files against the task's Files to Modify, Sensitive Patterns scans the diff for secrets, and Acceptance Criteria evaluates each criterion against the diff content independently of the eval results
assertion_results.evidence: Scope Containment (lines 29-37): compares PR files (style-conventions.md, SKILL.md) against task's Files to Modify with no reference to eval results. Sensitive Patterns (lines 60-66): scans added lines for 'passwords, API keys, tokens, private keys, environment files, cloud credentials, or database credentials' independently. Acceptance Criteria (lines 76-89): evaluates 7 of 7 criteria against diff content (Check 6 steps, output format, verdict mapping) with no reference to eval processing. All three checks operate on their domain-specific data without eval influence.
